creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
On 28 Feb, John Merryweather Cooper wrote:
> This commit (graphics/djvulibre) seems to have totally ignored my PR.
> :(
>
> The graphics/djvulibre port should have been named libdjvu++. It
> builds a subset of the same utilities (but no Mozilla plugin!).
> However, graphics/djvulibre is the AT&T code and NOT djvulibre in
> ANY sense. I'm sorry I've been slow to make the changes requested by
> lioux--I've been very ill until quite recently which has slowed me
> down. However, the graphics/djvulibre commit makes my life harder than
> necessary. :)
>
> Please rename the graphics/djvulibre commit.

graphics/djvulibre is a repo copy I requested -- as a maintainer -- of
the libdjvu++. I already have the new djvulibre port of my own ready to
commit, but was waiting for the repo copy. I looked for PRs on this, but
put libdjvu into the search field :\

Your version is very similar, but lacks the WITHOUT_X11 support, so I'll
commit mine :-) Would you like to become its maintainer? Thanks!

	-mi
